steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Akamai Functions is a microwave oven at every Akamai location worldwide. Drop in your code, it heats up in less than a millisecond, serves the meal, cools down. No warmup, no idle bill.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Akamai Functions is the function-as-a-service product built on the Spin/Wasmtime engine. Akamai acquired Fermyon (the company behind Spin) in December 2025, so what was "Fermyon Wasm Functions on Akamai" is now Akamai Functions.

Key traits:

- Sub-millisecond cold start (Spin apps boot in ~0.5ms)
- Tiny binaries (a Rust hello-world is ~280 KB)
- Wasmtime sandbox — stronger isolation than V8 isolates or containers
- Deploys globally across Akamai's core and distributed regions in seconds

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Generally available since November 2025, scaled to 75M requests/sec in production. Used by media, gaming, and commerce brands. Deploy with: `spin aka deploy`. Same Spin app runs locally, on Kubernetes (SpinKube), and on Akamai Functions — no rewrite.
"Akamai Functions = sub-millisecond Wasm serverless, globally distributed. One command deploys everywhere."

quiz.question: How fast is the Akamai Functions cold start?

options.explain: Containers take seconds; Wasm is in a different class.
Lambda cold starts in 100s of ms. Wasm is sub-millisecond.
Spin apps cold-start in ~0.5 ms. Effectively no cold-start tax.
It's sub-ms everywhere — the distributed runtime is the point.

quiz.question: Akamai acquired Fermyon in December 2025. What does that mean for the product roadmap?

options.text: The product is being shut down
options.explain: Opposite — it's being deepened with Akamai's platform integrations.

options.text: Spin is now proprietary and closed-source
options.explain: Spin and SpinKube remain open-source CNCF projects. Fermyon's co-founders continue to lead them inside Akamai.

options.text: Fermyon Wasm Functions becomes Akamai Functions, deeply integrated with Akamai's edge, security, and Inference Cloud
options.explain: The product is being rebranded and integrated with Akamai's broader stack — CDN, AAP, Inference Cloud, Object Storage.
No — it's its own runtime based on Wasmtime, not Lambda.
